text.title: Capacity Fit Calculator Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Capacity Fit Calculator. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Baseline velocity = median of last 4 sprints (not mean — outliers distort).
rule.rationale: Median is robust to one bad sprint; 4 sprints is the minimum window where rolling median is stable.

rule.statement: Focus factor cap = 0.75 for solo / small teams. Higher = optimism; lower = under-loading.
rule.rationale: Capacity-planning literature pins solo focus factor in 0.60-0.80 band; 0.75 is the safe centre.

rule.statement: Subtract explicit reductions: PTO days, holidays, planned ops days, on-call shifts.
rule.rationale: Implicit reductions are why sprints fail; making them explicit at planning time forces a real number.

rule.statement: If scope > computed capacity, cut scope — do not 'stretch'. Stretch goals are a planning antipattern.
rule.rationale: Stretch goals incentivise over-commit; they're the input to next sprint's burndown drift.

rule.statement: Capacity is recomputed each sprint, not carried over. Velocity changes; one stale capacity number is a trap.
rule.rationale: Velocity drifts ±20% across quarters; stale capacity = silent over-load.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-capacity-fit-calculator.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
